reasoning: The resolution requires the Federal Reserve to implement a sequence of Cut in September, Pause in October, and Cut in December. While the September 2025 meeting did result in a cut, futures markets correctly anticipate another cut at the upcoming October 28-29 meeting. As of October 24, the CME FedWatch tool indicated a 97-99% probability of an October rate cut. This overwhelming consensus for an October cut effectively rules out the 'Pause' required for the middle step of this sequence. Although data gaps from a potential government shutdown or dissenting cautious Fed officials introduce a very small risk of an unexpected pause, a probability over 1-3% is not justifiable when the meeting is just days away and market expectations are strongly cemented.
batch_queries: Federal Reserve interest rate outlook FOMC meetings 2025
recent US inflation and employment data impacting Fed rate path 2025
analyst forecasts Fed rate cuts Sept Oct Dec 2025
CME FedWatch tool probabilities late 2025 FOMC meetings
arguments against Fed rate cuts 2025 inflation resurgence risks
